$comment: Governance: written by Builder tier (Claude, 2026-07-15; authorized by Damien — lightweight path, see Ledger L-015). Revised 2026-07-15 per references/pm-feature-feedback.md (per-target sync state, tombstone handling, required-field tightening, hash-format constraint, spec kind deferred). Validates the tool-agnostic project-management export produced by the pm-export skill. The export is DERIVED from governance artifacts (work orders, capability map, ledger); those artifacts stay canonical. Field names and $id are codename-neutral per Constitution §1.3.
title: PM Export

schema.description: Format identifier and version. While below v1.0, treat any shape change (additive or breaking) as a minor bump — v0.1, v0.2, ... — since the contract has no consumer yet to protect. Reserve v1.0 for the first shape a real adapter implements against.

commit.description: Git commit the export was projected from (short or full sha, or 'working-tree' for an uncommitted run). Provenance for a derived artifact.

at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of generation. Optional — stamped by the human or harness, not asserted by the skill.

sources.description: Repo-relative paths the export was projected from (e.g. work-orders/, capability-map.md, ledger.md).

generated_from.description: Provenance. The export is derived — regenerate it from the sources rather than hand-editing item content.

status_model.description: Human-readable descriptor of where the canonical status vocabulary comes from. Informational.

items.description: The projected PM records. One per source artifact (work order, feature row, ledger entry).

id.description: Canonical repo id — the stable key across regenerations and syncs (e.g. WO-0001, F-015, L-014). Never reused.

kind.description: Item-type discriminator. epic = capability-map feature; task = work order; question / decision = ledger entry. A 'spec' kind for features/*.md is deliberately deferred — no feature-spec artifacts exist yet in this repo (see skills/pm-export/SKILL.md).

path.description: Repo-relative path the item was projected from.

artifact_class.description: Charter artifact class of the source (Work Order, Capability Map, Ledger, Feature Spec, ...).

anchor.description: Optional in-file anchor — the row id or entry id the item came from.

source.description: Where this item was projected from. Enables round-tripping a change back to the canonical doc.

body.description: Tool-neutral markdown description. Rendered into the target's description/body field at push time. Required — use an empty string if the source has no narrative content, never omit.

status.description: Canonical status. Work-order lifecycle (Constitution §8) + capability-map markers (queued, delivered) + ledger statuses (open, resolved, archived). This union is intentionally broad — the schema does not restrict which statuses are legal for which `kind`; that per-kind subset check (task -> WO states, epic -> queued/delivered/WO states, question|decision -> open/resolved/archived) is a semantic check run by the pm-export skill (Step 5), not schema-encoded, per Constitution §1.2 (validators are conveniences, not bundle dependencies). Target tools translate this via the per-tool mapping table in skills/pm-export/SKILL.md; the export itself stays tool-neutral.

priority.description: Optional tool-neutral priority. Null when the source declares none — do not invent one.

estimate.description: Optional tool-neutral estimate (points). Null when the source declares none.

labels.description: Tool-neutral tags, passed through to the target's label system. Empty array when none apply.

parent.description: Canonical id of the parent item (task -> its epic). Null for top-level items. Must be an id present in this export's items (checked by the skill's semantic-validation step, not this schema).

id.description: Canonical id of the related item. Must be an id present in this export's items — non-item references belong in `body`, not here (checked by the skill's semantic-validation step, not this schema).

relations.description: Typed links to other canonical ids in this export. Targets that lack native relations render these into the body. Empty array when none apply.

assignee.description: Tool-neutral assignee handle. Null when the source names none — resolved to a tool user at push time via a handle map, never invented here.

sync.description: SYNC STATE — the field the canonical docs do not hold. Keyed by target name (e.g. "github"); each value is that target's checkpoint. Matched by canonical id and PRESERVED across regeneration — never hand-clobber. Empty object `{}` means never synced to any target. An adapter updates its own key's `last_pushed_hash` only after a successful write, and its own key's `closed` only after successfully processing a closure — both are the adapter recording its OWN action, not reading the target's state back (the docs -> export -> tool flow stays one-way).

source_hash.description: Hash of the source slice at generation time, per the normalization spec in skills/pm-export/SKILL.md (UTF-8, CRLF normalized to LF, trailing whitespace/newline stripped, no addition). Drives per-target drift detection: an item is dirty for target T when source_hash != sync[T].last_pushed_hash. Null only if not yet computed.

present.description: OPTIONAL — omit when true (the common case: source still exists). Set to false only as a tombstone: the canonical source vanished but the item still has at least one non-empty `sync` entry, so it is retained (with its last-known title/body/status frozen) until every key in `sync` reaches `closed: true`. An item with an empty `sync` object that loses its source is dropped outright, never tombstoned — there is nothing to notify.

native.description: Escape hatch for source- or target-specific fields with no tool-neutral home, namespaced by owner (e.g. native.work_order). Required key, but the object itself may be empty ({}).

external_id.description: The id this target assigned on first push (e.g. a GitHub issue number).

last_pushed_hash.description: The item's source_hash as of the last successful write to this target. Compared against the current source_hash to decide whether this target needs an update. Null if the item was registered (e.g. matched by marker) but no push has completed yet.

closed.description: Set by this target's adapter after it successfully processes closure of a tombstoned item (present:false). Once every key in an item's sync object has closed:true, the next regeneration drops the item. Omit (defaults false) while open.
